channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Doug Ford vows to find out how Ontario’s jails improperly released more than 150 inmates
‘If we can’t build peace in Ontario, where can we build it?’ Leaders speak out against rising antisemitism at Holocaust remembrance event
item.description: Leaders from all four elected parties at Queen’s Park took part in a ceremony Tuesday to mark Yom Hashoah, a day to remember the Holocaust, and took the occasion to speak out against increasingly violent acts of antisemitism.

item.title: Controversial Ontario fund rejected TTC training program with little explanation: documents
item.description: The Ontario government rejected a program that would have helped 21 high school graduates learn trades at Toronto’s transit system using a boilerplate letter with little explanation, documents obtained by CTV News show.

item.title: Ontario to make attendance count for up to 15 per cent of high school final grades
item.description: Ontario high school students will soon have their attendance count for a sizable portion of their final grades, one of a set of upcoming changes to how students are assessed.

item.title: Ontario proposes ban on requiring workers to pay for uniforms
item.description: Ontario’s labour minister is proposing to ban employers from making employees pay for their uniforms.

item.title: Ontario introduces sweeping changes to education. Here’s what it means for students and their parents
item.description: The Ontario government will cut the number of trustees at the Toronto District School Board nearly in half and create new non-elected executive roles as part of a bill aimed at overhauling school governance in the province.

item.title: Ontario to boost injured worker benefits, extend coverage beyond age 65
item.description: Ontario workers who are injured on the job could soon receive higher loss-of-earnings benefits, under legislation the labour minister plans to introduce.

item.title: Ontario considering allowing legal cannabis stores to open earlier
item.description: Ontario cannabis stores could be allowed to open two hours earlier.

item.title: Ontario failed to collect nearly $10M owed by alleged animal abusers: documents
item.description: Ontario has failed to collect more than 90 per cent of money owed by alleged animal abusers whose pets, livestock or menagerie were seized during investigations, The Canadian Press has learned.

item.title: Ontario pulls funding for last provincially supported supervised consumption site
item.description: Ontario is pulling funding for the last remaining provincially funded supervised drug consumption site, after announcing funding cuts at seven others last month.

item.title: Ontario education minister says ‘significant’ legislation on school board changes coming Monday
item.description: Ontario Education Minister Paul Calandra says the government will be introducing “significant” legislation on school board governance on April 13.

item.title: Northlander train begins final testing before service resumes this year
item.description: The return of passenger rail service connecting northern Ontario to the south took another step forward Thursday as the Northlander began full testing of the routes and all systems needed before the trains are relaunched.

item.title: Ontario electricity system to add 14 new solar, wind projects
item.description: Ontario’s electricity system operator is giving long-term energy generation contracts to 12 new solar and two wind projects.

item.title: GTHA transit unions call on Ford government to halt expansion of fare integration program
item.description: Local transit unions in the Greater Toronto and Hamilton Area are calling on the Doug Ford government not to push forward with its plan to harmonize fares across the region, saying they have serious concerns about the move.

item.title: Ontario to extend WSIB coverage in privately run retirement and group homes
item.description: Ontario is planning to extend workplace safety and insurance coverage to health-care and support workers in privately run retirement homes and group homes.
